purpose: Top-level build definition. Declares the LightHost JUCE GUI app target, embeds the microphone tray icon as a binary-data target, links JUCE 8 modules, sets per-platform compile/link options, and exposes plugin-host flags (VST/VST3/AU) for HostStartup.cpp's static_assert.
invariants: project(LightHost VERSION 3.8.1) is the single source of truth for the app version — JUCE_APPLICATION_VERSION_STRING is derived from $<TARGET_PROPERTY:LightHost,JUCE_VERSION>.
CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD must remain 23 (or higher) — Source/ uses C++23 features.
JUCE_PLUGINHOST_VST3 / JUCE_PLUGINHOST_AU / JUCE_PLUGINHOST_VST must stay defined as 1 — HostStartup.cpp has a static_assert that fails the build otherwise.
CMAKE_SYSTEM_VERSION is forced to 10.0.26100.0 BEFORE project() so the VS generator picks it up as WindowsTargetPlatformVersion.
gotchas: NEEDS_CURL=FALSE and NEEDS_WEB_BROWSER=FALSE keep the Linux binary independent of libcurl/webkit2gtk-4.0; configure-time warnings about those missing packages on WSL are expected and benign.
VST2 SDK include path is hard-coded to lib/vstsdk2.4 — VST2 is forbidden on Windows by Steinberg, so the include is effectively macOS/Linux-only even though it's set unconditionally.
/wd5105 (MSVC) silences a C5105 warning that originates from JUCE's bundled HarfBuzz preprocessor macros — it does NOT suppress warnings in LightHost source.
The default and release presets in CMakePresets.json use the Visual Studio 18 2026 generator and fail on Linux/macOS; use ninja-* or clang-* presets there.
